reasoning_summary: The Frisch elasticity for prime-age workers is typically estimated in the range of 0.2-0.6 in modern empirical and calibration work. Micro-level behavioral responses to wage changes tend to be on the lower end (0.1-0.3), while macro-calibration exercises often require slightly higher values (0.4-0.6) to match aggregate labor supply responses. My central estimate of 0.4 reflects a balance between these traditions, with substantial uncertainty given methodological variation and debate in the literature about lifecycle dynamics and wealth effects.
